title: The Elvis Operator (?:)
description: Provide a default value when things go missing.

slides.title: Fallback Plans 🪂
slides.content:
## Avoiding Null Returns

The Safe Call `?.` safely returns `null` if the box is empty. But sometimes, we don't want `null` bleeding into our app. 

We want a guaranteed fallback fallback value if things go wrong.

slides.title: The Elvis Operator (?:) 🎸
slides.content:
Look at it sideways, it looks like Elvis Presley's hair! `?:`

The Elvis Operator translates to: "Use the value on the left. But if the left side is completely empty (null), use this default value on the right instead!"

quizData.question: If `val count: Int? = 5`, what will `val total = count ?: 0` result in?
quizData.explanation: Because the left side (`count`) was perfectly fine and contained a `5`, the Elvis operator ignores the right side fallback completely.

slides.title: The Ultimate Combo 🤜🤛
slides.content:
Combining Safe Calls (`?.`) with the Elvis Operator (`?:`) is the ultimate Kotlin superpower.

Try length. If it's empty, abort and give me 0 instead.
